result.architecture_package:
# Architecture Package — Compliance Management Platform

## A. Executive Summary
- Modernize legacy compliance tracker for 10,000 concurrent users across 14 bureaus, FedRAMP Moderate, ATO in under 9 months.
- Zero-trust, no unrestricted egress; authentication via agency LDAP with RBAC (admin / reviewer / viewer).
- Target architecture: modular web app, event-driven ingest from internal SFTP, AES-256 at rest, tamper-evident audit log, WCAG 2.1 AA UI.
- 30/60/90-day roadmap drives an MVP at 60 days and ATO-ready artifacts by day 90.
- AI use is narrow and human-in-the-loop: triage assistance and draft attestation summaries; no autonomous decisioning.
- Four items flagged as Government Decision Required (data classification, retention, LDAP schema, ATO boundary).

## B. Mission Problem Statement
Reviewers across 14 bureaus must triage incoming compliance artifacts, route them to SMEs, and produce signed attestations within agency SLAs. The legacy system cannot scale, lacks audit rigor, and blocks ATO renewal. The replacement must deliver the same workflow with higher throughput, stronger controls, and measurable traceability.

## C. Requirements Inventory
| ID | Title | Category | Priority | Source | Ambiguities | Dependencies |
|----|-------|----------|----------|--------|-------------|--------------|
| REQ-001 | RBAC with admin / reviewer / viewer | security | critical | requirements_text | Are bureau-scoped sub-roles required? | — |
| REQ-002 | AES-256 encryption at rest | security | critical | requirements_text | KMS provider? Customer-managed keys? | REQ-010 |
| REQ-003 | LDAP SSO | functional | high | CMP-412, requirements_text | 'should' — confirm mandatory; LDAP schema attributes? | REQ-001 |
| REQ-004 | PDF + CSV export | functional | medium | requirements_text | 508-compliant PDF tagging required? | REQ-006 |
| REQ-005 | Search < 2s at 1M records | performance | high | requirements_text | P50 or P95? Cold vs warm cache? | — |
| REQ-006 | WCAG 2.1 AA | accessibility | high | requirements_text | Testing tool and cadence? | — |
| REQ-007 | Audit trail with timestamps | security | critical | requirements_text | Retention duration? Immutable store? | REQ-001, REQ-010 |
| REQ-008 | 10k concurrent users, 14 bureaus | non-functional | high | SOW-2026-014 | Burst vs sustained? | REQ-005 |
| REQ-009 | SFTP-based artifact ingest, 5-min poll, 25 MB max | interoperability | high | ICD-ARTIFACT-INGEST | Failure / replay semantics? | — |
| REQ-010 | FedRAMP Moderate controls baseline | compliance | critical | context | Confirm inherited vs in-boundary controls | REQ-002, REQ-007 |
| REQ-011 | P2 incident workflow (page, snapshot, notify ISSO < 1h) | non-functional | high | RB-INCIDENT-P2 | Integrate with existing ServiceNow? | REQ-007 |

## D. Current-State Assessment
Only one code artifact was supplied (`legacy_exporter.py`). Observations:
- Writes CSV to `/tmp`, no auth, no audit, no encoding handling, no access check — not reusable as-is.
- No current-state diagram, DB schema, or runtime topology was provided.
- Recommendation: treat exporter as throwaway and re-implement behind the new service boundary.
State: **Partial — replace legacy exporter, current architecture otherwise undocumented.**

## E. Target-State Architecture
```
         ┌──────────────────┐      ┌───────────────────┐
 Agency  │  LDAP / IdP       │◀────▶│  Auth Service     │
 users ──▶  (existing)       │      │  (OIDC broker)    │
         └──────────────────┘      └────────┬──────────┘
                                            │ JWT
                           ┌────────────────▼────────────────┐
                           │  Web UI (WCAG 2.1 AA, React)     │
                           └────────────────┬────────────────┘
                                            │ HTTPS / mTLS
                           ┌────────────────▼────────────────┐
                           │  API Gateway + RBAC policy      │
                           └──┬──────────┬──────────┬────────┘
                              │          │          │
                     ┌────────▼──┐  ┌────▼─────┐ ┌──▼──────────┐
                     │ Workflow  │  │ Search   │ │ Export      │
                     │ Service   │  │ Service  │ │ Service     │
                     └────┬──────┘  └────┬─────┘ └─────┬───────┘
                          │              │             │
                     ┌────▼──────────────▼─────────────▼────┐
                     │ Postgres (AES-256) + OpenSearch       │
                     │ + Object Store (AES-256, KMS)         │
                     └───────────────┬───────────────────────┘
                                     │
                     ┌───────────────▼───────────────────────┐
                     │ Append-only Audit Log (WORM / signed) │
                     └───────────────────────────────────────┘

 Internal SFTP ─▶ Ingest Worker (5-min poll) ─▶ Workflow Service
 Observability: OTEL traces + metrics, logs shipped to SIEM
 CI/CD: GitHub Actions → signed artifacts → gated deploy to GovCloud
```
- **Integration patterns**: event-driven ingest (SFTP poller), synchronous request/response for UI, async job queue for exports.
- **Deployment**: GovCloud Kubernetes, namespace-per-bureau optional, private VPC, no public egress.
- **CI/CD + observability**: signed container images, SBOM, policy-as-code gates, OTEL to agency SIEM, dashboards per SLO.

## F. Risks / Unknowns / Government Decisions
| # | Item | Type | Action |
|---|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Data classification of ingested artifacts | Government Decision Required | Confirm CUI vs PII vs public |
| 2 | Audit retention duration | Government Decision Required | Confirm with ISSO (7y assumed) |
| 3 | LDAP attribute mapping to RBAC roles | Government Decision Required | Request directory schema |
| 4 | ATO boundary and inherited controls | Government Decision Required | Align with agency CSO |
| 5 | SFTP replay / DLQ semantics | Risk | Define idempotency keys |
| 6 | 2s P95 search at 1M records | Risk | Validate with load test in Sprint 2 |
| 7 | Cross-bureau data isolation | Risk | Tenancy design decision |

## G. AI Suitability Analysis
| Capability | AI fit? | Rationale |
|------------|---------|-----------|
| Artifact classification / routing | **Good** | Human-reviewable suggestions; always with reviewer override. |
| Draft attestation summaries | **Good** | LLM drafts, SME signs. Require citation to source artifact. |
| RBAC / authZ decisions | **No** | Deterministic policy engine — not LLM. |
| Audit log analysis | **Maybe** | Offline anomaly detection only; not in enforcement path. |
| Search ranking | **Maybe** | Start with BM25 / vector hybrid; defer generative answers. |
- All AI features require explainability metadata, prompt + output logging, and an opt-out.
- No autonomous decisioning; no egress to external model APIs without ATO.

## H. Backlog with Priorities
- **EPIC-1 Identity & Access (Critical)**
  - F-1.1 LDAP OIDC broker — AC: reviewer logs in with agency creds; JWT includes role claim.
  - F-1.2 RBAC policy service — AC: deny-by-default; policy change audited.
- **EPIC-2 Secure Data Platform (Critical)**
  - F-2.1 Postgres + object store with KMS-backed AES-256 — AC: data unreadable without KMS.
  - F-2.2 Append-only audit log — AC: tamper detection alarm on hash mismatch.
- **EPIC-3 Reviewer Workflow (High)**
  - F-3.1 Triage queue — AC: reviewer can claim, route, and attest.
  - F-3.2 WCAG 2.1 AA UI — AC: axe-core CI gate passes on all routes.
- **EPIC-4 Ingest & Interop (High)**
  - F-4.1 SFTP poller with 25 MB limit — AC: malformed payload lands in DLQ with reason.
- **EPIC-5 Search & Export (High / Medium)**
  - F-5.1 Hybrid search — AC: P95 < 2s at 1M records in load test.
  - F-5.2 PDF + CSV export — AC: tagged PDF passes PAC checker.
- **EPIC-6 Observability & IR (High)**
  - F-6.1 SIEM + OTEL — AC: P2 runbook auto-triggers ServiceNow ticket.
- **EPIC-7 AI Assist (Enhancement)**
  - F-7.1 Triage suggestion — AC: shown as suggestion only, never auto-applied.
  - F-7.2 Draft summary — AC: cites every claim to a source artifact.

## I. Traceability Matrix
| Mission Need | Requirement | Architecture Decision | Backlog |
|--------------|-------------|-----------------------|---------|
| Scale to 10k users / 14 bureaus | REQ-008 | Stateless services + HPA in GovCloud K8s | EPIC-3, EPIC-6 |
| Single sign-on | REQ-003 | OIDC broker in front of LDAP | F-1.1 |
| Least-privilege access | REQ-001 | Central policy service, deny-by-default | F-1.2 |
| Protect data | REQ-002, REQ-010 | KMS + AES-256 everywhere at rest | F-2.1 |
| Auditability | REQ-007 | Append-only, signed audit log | F-2.2 |
| Reviewer productivity | CONOPS | Triage queue + SME routing | F-3.1 |
| Inclusive access | REQ-006 | WCAG gate in CI | F-3.2 |
| Reliable ingest | REQ-009 | SFTP poller + DLQ | F-4.1 |
| Fast search | REQ-005 | Hybrid BM25 + vector | F-5.1 |
| Exportable reports | REQ-004 | Export worker with tagged PDF | F-5.2 |
| Incident response | REQ-011 | SIEM + runbook automation | F-6.1 |

## J. Recommended First Sprint
**Goal**: prove the secure backbone end-to-end with a thin vertical slice.
- Stand up GovCloud K8s namespace, private VPC, KMS, baseline IaC.
- Implement OIDC ↔ LDAP broker (F-1.1) with one test reviewer account.
- Ship RBAC policy skeleton (F-1.2) with three roles.
- Provision Postgres with KMS-backed AES-256 (F-2.1 partial).
- Wire OTEL traces + centralized logs to SIEM (F-6.1 partial).
- Decide open Government Decision items #1–#4.
**Exit criteria**: a reviewer can log in via LDAP, hit a protected `/ping` endpoint, and the audit log records the call with a signed timestamp.
